subject: American Engineerin g Standards Committee
evidence: In 1920 , the American Engineerin g Standards Committee [later the American St andards Associations (ASA)] organized Sectional Committ ee B16 to unify and further develop standards for pipe flanges and fitt ings (and later for valves and gaskets).

subject: Sectional Committ ee B16
evidence: Cosponsors of the B16 Committ ee were ASME, the Heat ing and Piping Contractors Nat ional Associat ion [now Mechanical Contractors Association of America (MCAA)], and the Manufacturers Standardization Society of the Valves and Fittings Industry (MSS).

subject: Sectional Committ ee B16
evidence: Cosponsors of the B16 Committ ee were ASME, the Heat ing and Piping Contractors Nat ional Associat ion [now Mechanical Contractors Association of America (MCAA)], and the Manufacturers Standardization Society of the Valves and Fittings Industry (MSS).

subject: Sectional Committ ee B16
object: Manufacturers Standardization Society of the Valves and Fittings Industry (MSS)
evidence: Cosponsors of the B16 Committ ee were ASME, the Heat ing and Piping Contractors Nat ional Associat ion [now Mechanical Contractors Association of America (MCAA)], and the Manufacturers Standardization Society of the Valves and Fittings Industry (MSS).

subject: ASME B16.5-1953
evidence: Approval by Sectional Committee, cosponsors, and ASA result ed in the publication of ASA B16.5-1953 (designation changed from B16e).

subject: Subcommittee 3
evidence: Subcommittee 3 then began a revision of the entire standard.

subject: the_1949_Supplement
evidence: Technically, the 1949 Supplement was absorbed, new materials were recognized, a general rating method w as developed and added as an appendix, and welding end preparations were expanded.

subject: new_materials
evidence: Technically, the 1949 Supplement was absorbed, new materials were recognized, a general rating method w as developed and added as an appendix, and welding end preparations were expanded.

evidence: Technically, the 1949 Supplement was absorbed, new materials were recognized, a general rating method w as developed and added as an appendix, and welding end preparations were expanded.

evidence: Technically, the 1949 Supplement was absorbed, new materials were recognized, a general rating method w as developed and added as an appendix, and welding end preparations were expanded.

subject: ASME B16.5-1961
evidence: The more modest revision app roved as ASA B16.5-1961 changed the t ext to clarify the inten t or to make requirements easier to administer.

subject: ASME B16.5
evidence: Based on the Subcommitt ee 4 report, the rating pr ocedure was revised, and a rating basis for Class 150 (150 lb) flanges was developed.

subject: ANSI B16.5-1973
evidence: Following final approval on October 23, the Standard w as published as ANSI B16.5-1973.

subject: ASME B16.5-1977
evidence: ANSI B16.5-1977, Steel Pipe Flanges and Flanged Fittings, was published on June 16, 1977.

evidence: Final approval was granted for ANSI B16.5-1984, Pipe Flanges and Flanged Fittings on August 14.

evidence: The 1996 Edit ion was app roved by ANSI on October 3, 1996 , with the new designat ion ASME B16.5-1996.

subject: ASME Standards
evidence: ASME Standards are developed and maintained with the intent to represent the consensus of concerned interests.

subject: users of this Standard
evidence: users of this Standard may interact with the Committee by requesting interpretations, proposing revisions, and attending Committee meetings.

subject: Correspondence
object: Secretary, B16 Standards Committee, The American Society of Mechanical Engineers, Two Park Avenue, New York, NY 10016-5990
evidence: Corre- spondence should be addressed to: Secretary, B16 Standards Committee The American Society of Mechanical Engineers Two Park Avenue New York, NY 10016-5990

subject: inquiries
evidence: As an alternative, inquiries may be submitted via e-mail to: SecretaryB16@asme.org.

subject: Revisions
evidence: Revisions are made periodically to the Standard to incorporate changes that appear necessary or desirable, as demonstrated by the experience gained from the application of the Standard.

subject: The Committee
evidence: The Committ ee welcomes proposals for revisions to this Standar d.

subject: Cases
evidence: Cases may be issued for the purpose of providing alternative rules when justified, to permit early implementation of an approved revision when the need is urgent, or to provide rules not covered by existing provisions.

subject: Requests_for_Cases
object: Statement of Need and Background Information
evidence: Requests for Cases shall provide a Statement of Need and Background Information.

subject: B16 Committ ee
evidence: Upon request, the B16 Committ ee will render an interpretation of any require-ment of the Standar d.

subject: request_for_interpretation
evidence: The request for interpretation should be clear and unambiguous.

subject: inquirer
object: Subject: Cite the applicable paragraph number(s) and the topic of the inquiry. Edition: Cite the applicable edition of the Standard for which the interpretation is being requested. Question: Phrase the question as a request for an interpretation of a specific requirement suitable for general understanding and use, not as a request for an approval of a proprietary design or situation.
evidence: It is further recommended that the inquirer submit his/her request in the following format:

subject: ASME_procedures
evidence: ASME procedur es p rovide for reconsideration of any interpretation when or if additional information that might affect an interpretation is available.

subject: asme_b16_5_2013
evidence: 1.2 References Codes, standards, and specifications, containing pro- visions to the ex t ent ref eren ce d h erei n, co ns t i tu t e requirements of this Standar d.

subject: asme_b16_5_2013
evidence: 1.2 References Codes, standards, and specifications, containing pro- visions to the ex t ent ref eren ce d h erei n, co ns t i tu t e requirements of this Standar d. These reference docu- ments are listed in Mandatory Appendix III.

subject: asme_b16_5_2013
evidence: 1.2 References Codes, standards, and specifications, containing pro- visions to the ex t ent ref eren ce d h erei n, co ns t i tu t e requirements of this Standar d. These reference docu- ments are listed in Mandatory Appendix III. 1.3 Time of Purchase, Manufactur e, or Installation The pressure–temperature ratings in this Standard are applicable upon its publication to all flanges and flanged fitt ing s within its scope, which otherwise meet it s requirements .

subject: asme_b16_5_2013
evidence: 1.4 User Accountability This Standard cites duties and responsibilities that are to be assumed by the flange or flanged fitting user in the areas of, for example, application, installation, system hydrostatic testing, operation, and material selection.

subject: asme_b16_5_2013
evidence: 1.5 Quality Systems Requirements relating to the product manufacturer ’s Quality Syst em Program are described in Nonmandatory Appendix D.

subject: asme_b16_5_2013
evidence: 1.6 Relevant Units This Standard stat es values in both SI (Metric) and U.S. Customary units. As an exception, diameter of bolts and flange bolt holes are expressed in inch units only . These systems of units are to be regarded separately as standard.

subject: asme_b16_5_2013
evidence: 1.6 Relevant Units This Standard stat es values in both SI (Metric) and U.S. Customary units. As an exception, diameter of bolts and flange bolt holes are expressed in inch units only . These systems of units are to be regarded separately as standard.

subject: flanged_joints
evidence: Pressure–temperature ratings apply to flanged joints that conform to the limitations on bolting in para. 5.3 and on gaskets in para. 5.4, which are made up in accordance with good practice for alignment and assembly

subject: mixed_flanged_joints
evidence: If the t wo flanges in a flanged joint do not have the same pressure–temperature rating, the rating of the joint at any temperature is the lower of the t wo flange ratings at that temperatur e.

subject: temperature_of_pressure_containing_shell
evidence: In general, this temperature is the same as that of the contained fluid.

subject: temperature_below_minus_29_celsius
evidence: For any temperature below −29°C ( −20°F), the rating shall be no gr eater than the rating shown for −29°C ( −20°F)

subject: NPS
object: pipe, flange, or flanged fitting end connection size identification
evidence: "the use of the phrase \"nominal pipe size\" or the designation NPS followed by a dimensionless number is for the purpose of pipe, flange, or flanged fitt ing end connection size identifica- tion."

subject: reducing_fittings
evidence: "Reducing fittings shall be designated by the NPS for the openings in the sequence indicat ed in the sketches of Fig. 5."

subject: reducing_flanges
evidence: "Reducing flanges shall be designat ed by the NPS for each opening."

subject: flanges_and_flanged_fittings
evidence: "Except as modified herein, flanges and flanged fittings shall be marked as requir ed in MSS SP-25, except as noted in para. 4.2."

subject: manufacturer
evidence: "The manufacturer \'s name or trade- mark shall be applied."

subject: ASME B16.5-2013
evidence: The designation B16 or B16.5 shall be applied to the flange or flanged fitting, prefera- bly located adjacent to the class designation, to indicate conformanc e to this Standard.

subject: ASME B16.5-2013
evidence: Temperature markings are not required on flanges or flanged fitt ings; however, if marked, the temperature shall be shown with its corres- ponding tabulated pressure rating for the material.

subject: ASME B16.5-2013
evidence: The NPS designation shall be marked on flanges and flanged fittings. Reducing flanges and reducing flanged fittings shall be marked with the appli- cable NPS designations as required by paras. 3.2 and 3.3.

subject: ASME B16.5-2013
evidence: The edge (periphery) of each ring joint flange shall be marked with the lett er R and the corresponding ring groove number.

subject: ASME B16.5-2013
evidence: Material for com- ponents that meet the requirements for more than one specification or grade of a specification listed in Table 1A may, at the manufacturer ’s option, be marked with more than one of the applicable specification or grade sym- bols. These identification markings shall be placed so as to avoid confusion in identification. The multiple marking shall be in accordance with the guidelines set out in ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code, Section II, Part D, Appendix 7.

subject: ASME B16.5-2013
evidence: Materials required for flanges and flanged fittings are listed in Table 1A with the restriction that plat e materials shall be used only for blind flanges and reduc- ing flanges without hubs.

subject: ASME B16.5-2013
evidence: Recommended bolt ing mat erials are list ed in Table 1B (see para. 5.3).

subject: Bolting listed in Table 1B
evidence: Bolting listed in Table 1B is recommended for use in flanged joints covered by this Standard.

subject: Corresponding_materials_listed_in_Section_II_of_the_ASME_Boiler_and_Pressure_Vessel_Code
evidence: Corresponding materials listed in Section II of the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code may be used provided that the requirements of the ASME specification are identical to or more stringent than the ASTM specification for the Grade, Class, or type of material.

subject: Lapped Joint Flanges
evidence: Lapped joint flanges shall be furnished with flat faces as illustrat ed i n Tables 8, 11, 14, 16, 18, 20, and 22 (Tables II-8, II-11, II-14, II-16, II-18, II-20, and II-22 of Mandatory Appendix II).

subject: Lap Joint Stub Ends
object: Fig. 6 (Fig. II-6 of Mandatory Appendix II) and paras. 6.4.3.1 through 6.4.3.3
evidence: Lap joint stub ends shall be in accordance with Fig. 6 (Fig. II-6 of Mandatory Appendix II) and paras. 6.4.3.1 through 6.4.3.3.

subject: Raised Face
evidence: The finished thickness of the lap shall be no less than nominal pipe wall thickness.

subject: Large Male and Female
evidence: The finishe d height of the male face shall be the gr eater of the wall thickness of the pipe used or 7 mm (0.25 in.).

subject: Thickness_of_lap_remaining_after_machining_the_female_face
evidence: The thick- ness of lap that remains after machining the female face shall be no less than the nomina l wall thickness of pipe used.

subject: Thickness_of_lap_remaining_after_machining_the_tongue_or_groove_face
evidence: The thickness of the lap remaining after machining the tongue or groove face shall be no less than the nominal wall thickness of the pipe used.

subject: Thickness_of_lap_remaining_after_machining_the_ring_groove
evidence: The thickness of th e la p remaining aft er machining the ring groo ve shall be no less than the nominal wall thickness of pipe used.

subject: Outside_diameters_of_lap_for_ring_joins
object: Table 5 (Table II-5 of Mandatory Appendix II), dimension K
evidence: The outside diameters of the lap for ring join ts are shown in Table 5 (Table II-5 of Mandatory Appendix II), dimen- sion K.

subject: Outside_diameters_of_laps_for_large_female_large_tongue_and_groove_and_small_tongue_and_groove
object: Table 4 (Tabl e II-4 of Mandatory Appendix II)
evidence: The outside diameters of laps for large female, large tongue and groove, and small tongue and groove are show n in Table 4 (Tabl e II-4 of Mandatory Appendix II).

subject: Blind Flanges
evidence: Blind flanges need not be faced in the center if, when this center part is raised, its diame- ter is at least 25 mm (1 in.) smaller than the inside diameter of fittings of the corresponding pressure class, as given in Tables 9 and 12 (Tables II-9 and II-12) or 25 mm (1 in.) smaller than the mating pipe inside diame- ter.

subject: Diameter_of_the_center_part_when_depressed
evidence: When the center part is depressed, its diameter shall not be gr eater than the inside diamet er of the corres- ponding pressure class fittings, as given in Tables 9 and 12 (Tables II-9 and II-12 of Mandatory Appendix II).

subject: gasket_contact_faces
evidence: The finish of the gasket contact faces shall be judged by visual comparison with Ra standards

subject: gasket_contact_surface_finish_tongue_and_groove_small_male_and_female
evidence: The gasket contact surface finish shall not exceed 3.2 f.Lm (125 f.Lin.) roughness.

subject: side_wall_surface_finish_ring_joint
evidence: The side wall surface finish of the gasket groove shall not exceed 1.6 f.Lm (63 f.Lin.) roughness.

subject: other_flange_facing
evidence: Either a serrated concentric or serrated spiral finish having a resultant surface finish from 3.2 f.Lm to 6.3 f.Lm (125 f.Lin. to 250 f.Lin.) average roughness shall be furnished.

subject: imperfections_in_flange_facing_finish
evidence: Imperfections in the flange facing finish shall not exceed the dimensions shown in Table 3

subject: bolt_holes
evidence: Bolt holes shall be equally spaced, and pairs of bolt holes shall straddle fitting centerlines.

subject: bolting_bearing_surfaces
evidence: Flanges and flanged fittings shall have bearing surfaces for bolting that are parallel to the flange face within 1 deg.

subject: ASME B16.5-2013
evidence: Tables 7, 10, 13, 15, 17, 19, and 21 (Tables II-7, II-10, II-13, II-15, II-17, II-19, and II-21 of Copyright ASME International Provided by IHS under license with ASME

subject: stud_bolt_length
evidence: Stud bolt lengths, including t he height of t wo heavy hexagon nuts, are shown as dimension L in Tables 7, 10, 13, 15, 17, 19, and 21 (Tables II-7, II-10, II-13, II-15, II-17, II-19, and II-21 of Copyright ASME International Provided by IHS under license with ASME

subject: stud_bolt_length
evidence: The tabulated stud bolt length L does not include the height of end points. An end point is defined as an un threaded length, such as a chamfer, which extends beyond the thread.

subject: end_point
evidence: An end point is defined as an un threaded length, such as a chamfer, which extends beyond the thread.

subject: bolting_recommendation
evidence: For flange joints, stud bolts with a nut at each end are recommended for all applications and especially for high temperature service.

subject: external taper threads in ASME B1.20.1
evidence: The length of external taper threads in ASME B1.20.1 is sufficient to provide a satisfactory joint when assem- bled with corresponding internal taper threads in cou- plings and fitt ings.

subject: length of internal thread in Classes 150, 300, and 400 flanges
evidence: In this St andard, the length o f internal thread in Classes 150, 300, and 400 flanges also conforms to ASME B1.20.1.

subject: length through the hub in Class 600 and higher rated flanges
object: the length for internal threads in ASME B1.20.1
evidence: In Class 600 and higher rat ed flanges, the lengt h through the hub m ay exceed the length for internal th read s in ASME B1.20.1 .

subject: extended length of internal threads in Class 600 and higher rated flanges
object: the taper of the standard taper threads
evidence: When this occurs , th e extended length of internal threads follows the taper of the standard taper threads.

subject: pipe end of threaded flanges conforming to this Standard
object: the mating surface of the flange
evidence: To meet this inten t, the following requirements are imposed on the mating pipe and exter- nal thread:

subject: Schedule 80 or heavier pipe
object: flanges of Class 600 or higher rating
evidence: Pipe to be threaded in to flanges of Class 600 or higher rating shall be Schedule 80 or heavier in wall thickness.

subject: external effective thread length on the pipe end
evidence: The length of external effective thread on the pipe end shall be gr eater than specified in ASME B1.20.1.

subject: pipe end
object: the gage by the distance specified in Table I- 1
evidence: When tested with the standard ring gage, the pipe end shall project beyond the gage by the distance specified in Table I- 1, subject to a tolerance of one thread pitch as provided in ASME B1.20.1.

subject: extra threads
evidence: The extra threads shall continue the taper specified in ASME B1.20.1, so that the pitch diameter of the thread at the pipe end is less than specified therein.

subject: A
evidence: A p nominal outside diameter of pipe

subject: B
evidence: B p nominal inside diameter of pipe

subject: ASTM A217 Gr. C12
evidence: Use normalized and tempered material only.

subject: ASTM A217 Gr. C12
evidence: The deliberate addition of any element not listed in ASTM A217, Table 1 is prohibited, except that calcium (Ca) and manganese (Mn) may be added for deoxidation.

subject: pressure-temperature ratings
evidence: Pressure–temperature ratings for this Standard have been determined by the pr ocedures described in this Nonmandatory Appendix.

subject: primary considerations
object: component dimensions and material properties to sustain pressure and other loads
evidence: The primary considerations in establishing ratings are componen t dimension s and material properties to sustain pressure and other loads.

subject: total flange bolting cross-sectional area requirements
evidence: Total flange bolting cross-sectional area requirements are based on the following relationship: PcAg Ab ≥ 7000 (1)

subject: flanged fitting wall thickness requirements
evidence: Wall thickness requirements fo r flanged fittings are specified in para. 6.1.

subject: A-2.3 Method
object: Class 300 and higher of materials corresponding to those in Materials Groups 2 and 3 of Table 1A
evidence: Pressure –t emperature rat ings for Class 300 an d higher, of materials corresponding to those in Materials Groups 2 and 3 of Table 1A, are established by the method of paras. A-2.1 and A-2.2
that in paras. A-2.2(a)(1) and A-2.2(a)(2), the 60% factor shall be changed to 70%

subject: creep_range
evidence: For Group 2 materials, the creep range is consider ed to be at temperatures in excess of 510°C (950°F) unless the material properties indicate lower temperatures should be used

subject: creep_range_onset_temperature
evidence: For Group 3 materials, the creep range onset temperature shall be determined on an individual basis

subject: pressure_temperature_ratings
evidence: Pressure–temperature ratings for Class 150 rating des- ignation are established by the method given for the related materials in paras. A-2.1, A-2.2, and A-2.3

subject: Pr, the pressure_class_rating_index_in_eq._(3)_for_Class_150
evidence: The value of Pr, the pressure class rating index in eq. (3) for Class 150, shall be 115.

subject: S1, the selected_stress_MPa_(psi)_for_the_specific_material_at_temperature_T
object: either para. A-2.1 or A-2.2, as applicable
evidence: The value for S1, the select ed stress MPa (psi), for the specific material at temperatur e T shall be in accordance with the requirements of either para. A-2.1 or A-2.2, as applicable.

subject: pt, the rated_working_pressure_bar_(psi)_for_Class_150

subject: T
evidence: The value of T in eq. (4) shall not exceed 538° C (1,000°F).

subject: T
evidence: For values of T less than 38°C (100°F), use T equal to 38°C (100°F) in eq. (4).
